content: Most existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms focus on introducing multiple external or contrasting perspectives to improve reflection quality, while Self-Refine demonstrates that a single LLM can achieve effective iterative improvement without additional training or data via repeated self-feedback. We can hypothesize that augmenting the vanilla Self-Refine framework with multi-perspective inconsistent sampling from the LLM's own output distribution will create a stronger hybrid self-reflection system that retains Self-Refine's advantage of no extra training or supervision while addressing its limitation of single-perspective blind spots.
